title: NOC 12013 – Supervisors, Supply Chain, Tracking and Scheduling Coordination Occupations | GTR Immigration
description: Supervisors working in logistics, inventory control, dispatching, and scheduling roles are critical to keeping operations running smoothly across Canada. Under NOC 12013, these professionals benefit from growing labour-market demand and strong eligibility for skilled worker immigration programs leading to Permanent Residence (PR).

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Supervise shipping, receiving, storage, and inventory operations
Oversee purchasing transactions and production scheduling
Ensure compliance with safety, security, and regulatory standards
Resolve operational issues and customer complaints
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- to three-year post-secondary program in business, supply chain, production, or manufacturing management
Several years of experience in the supervised occupation
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 20,500 new positions for Supervisors, Supply Chain, Tracking and Scheduling Coordination Occupations, compared with 15,700 expected job seekers—creating favourable opportunities for skilled newcomers.
additional_information: Supervisors in supply chain and coordination roles are actively recruited across both public institutions and private-sector employers.

Expanding logistics and distribution sectorsStable supervisory-level employmentEligibility under Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration pathwaysAnnual salaries typically ranging from $34,600 to $83,300
